summary: The CI job failed during the C++ build phase because clang++ rejected an explicit template instantiation in the SIMD/moments code. Two compilation units failed with the same error: 'explicit instantiation of operator() does not refer to a function template, variable template, member function, member class, or static data member', causing ninja to abort and pip metadata generation to fail.
root_cause: clang++ (arm64-apple-darwin, with --werror) rejects the explicit instantiation of 'operator()' in pandas/simd/moments_simd.hpp:255 and pandas/_libs/simd/moments_inst_neon.cpp:14 because the target entity is not a function template, variable template, member function, member class, or static data member. This causes both libmoments_simd_neon.a and libmoments_base.a compilation units to fail.

evidence.quote: ../../pandas/_libs/include/pandas/simd/moments_simd.hpp:255:50: error: explicit instantiation of 'operator()' does not refer to a function template, variable template, member function, member class, or static data member
evidence.reason: This is the primary compiler error in the header file causing the build to fail.

evidence.quote: ../../pandas/_libs/simd/moments_inst_neon.cpp:14:43: error: explicit instantiation of 'operator()' does not refer to a function template, variable template, member function, member class, or static data member
evidence.reason: The same error appears in the NEON-specific instantiation file, confirming the explicit instantiation is malformed or targets a non-eligible entity.
Confirms the NEON SIMD compilation unit failed due to the compiler error.
Confirms the base moments compilation unit also failed with the same underlying error.
Warnings are treated as errors, meaning even a warning-level diagnostic about the explicit instantiation would be fatal; the error is a hard error regardless.
Ninja aborted the entire build after the compiler errors, propagating the failure upstream to pip.
The downstream consequence of the build failure: pip could not generate package metadata because the build subprocess exited with an error.

suggested_fix: Fix the explicit instantiation of 'operator()' at pandas/_libs/include/pandas/simd/moments_simd.hpp:255 and pandas/_libs/simd/moments_inst_neon.cpp:14 — either correct the syntax so it targets a valid function template/member function, or replace the explicit instantiation with an appropriate alternative (e.g., an explicit template instantiation of the enclosing class or a free function template) that clang++ accepts under C++20.

model_info._backfill_note: Pre-Codex-2026-05-14 F2 fix. The Claude shim now persists _model_info from every API call; this row predates that emit so resolved_model and usage are not recoverable. requested_model is set to the shim-level alias (haiku for v1, sonnet for v2) which is what the post-fix shim emits.
